# AKT Navigator by Medexia

> AKT Navigator is an independent, audio-first revision resource for the
> MRCGP Applied Knowledge Test (AKT) — the RCGP-administered exam that UK
> GP trainees sit during specialty training. It offers free syllabus-mapped
> questions, mock exams with AI debriefs, and 90+ hours of audio revision
> covering all 32 RCGP curriculum topics. It is not affiliated with, or
> endorsed by, the Royal College of General Practitioners.

Key facts:
- The AKT is a 160-question multiple-choice exam taken over 2 hours 40
  minutes at Pearson VUE test centres (format updated October 2025).
- The current AKT format is 160 single-best-answer questions in 2 hours
  40 minutes: 80% clinical knowledge, 10% evidence-based practice and
  10% primary care organisation and management. There is no negative
  marking, and the pass mark varies by sitting.
- AKT exam day is managed through Pearson VUE test centres. Candidates
  should check their Pearson confirmation, bring the RCGP-required ID
  documents, arrive on time and request reasonable adjustments through
  the RCGP process before the published deadline.
- Remaining UK AKT sittings in 2026: 7 July 2026 and 26 October 2026.
  October final booking deadline: 1 September 2026. October Pearson
  centre booking window: 7-16 September 2026. October results: 25
  November 2026 at 17:00.
- The current RCGP AKT examination fee is £481. AKT eligibility is
  limited to ST2 and ST3 GP trainees. Attempt limits are four for
  trainees already in training on 1 August 2023, or six for trainees
  entering GP specialty training for the first time on or after 2
  August 2023.
- The AKT pass rate and pass mark vary by sitting. Official RCGP
  reports show 80.22% of all candidates passed in April 2026 with a
  107/160 pass mark, 76.52% passed in January 2026 with a 108/160
  pass mark, and 70.63% passed in October 2025 with a 109/160 pass
  mark.
- AKT results are released on sitting-specific RCGP result dates. If a
  trainee is unsuccessful, the score report, pass mark, official
  feedback reports, timed mocks and weak-area review should shape the
  retake plan.
- The exam covers clinical medicine (80%), evidence-based practice and
  statistics (10%), and organisational/professional topics (10%).
- The AKT syllabus maps to the RCGP GP curriculum and 32 topic guides,
  grouped into Professional, Life Stages and Clinical areas.
- AKT statistics revision should cover sensitivity, specificity, PPV,
  NPV, absolute and relative risk, NNT, NNH, confidence intervals,
  forest plots, prescribing charts, practice data and common study
  design terminology.
- Core AKT statistics formulas include sensitivity, specificity, PPV,
  NPV, absolute risk, relative risk, absolute risk reduction, absolute
  risk increase, NNT and NNH.
- AKT prescribing revision should cover medication monitoring,
  prescribing errors, side effects and interactions, repeat prescribing
  systems, higher-risk groups and BNF or NICE CKS checking.
- AKT confidentiality and safeguarding revision should cover GMC
  confidentiality principles, information sharing without consent,
  Gillick competence, Fraser guidance, recorded consultations, secure
  health-record handling and data-protection themes.
- AKT neurology revision should prioritise gait disturbance, headache
  red flags, first seizures, epilepsy medication principles, stroke and
  TIA recognition, Parkinson's disease, tremor, MS, neuropathy and
  urgent referral thresholds.
- AKT children and young people revision should prioritise acute
  illness recognition, paediatric cancer red flags, safeguarding,
  developmental milestones, immunisation, childhood infections,
  adolescent health, confidentiality and Fraser guidance.
- AKT dermatology revision should prioritise rashes and eruptions,
  eczema, psoriasis, acne, fungal infections, cellulitis, shingles,
  urticaria, leg ulcers, benign skin lesions, skin cancer recognition
  and urgent referral thresholds.
- AKT minor illness and urgent care revision should cover sepsis
  recognition, NEWS2 as an adjunct to clinical judgement, delayed or
  back-up antibiotics, acute otitis media, tonsillitis, sinusitis, UTI
  red flags, safety-netting and antimicrobial stewardship.
- AKT mental health revision should cover depression severity, anxiety
  and panic, suicide risk, self-harm, SSRI prescribing, psychosis,
  antipsychotic monitoring, eating disorders, safeguarding, the Mental
  Health Act and the Mental Capacity Act.
- Recent RCGP AKT feedback reports repeatedly highlight neurology,
  prescribing and medication monitoring, evidence/statistics chart
  interpretation, confidentiality/data protection, and children or
  safeguarding as useful weak-area revision signals.
- A practical AKT revision plan is around 12 weeks if possible. Eight
  weeks can work with an immediate baseline mock, daily question
  practice, timed mocks and focused weak-area review; four weeks needs
  strict triage and active recall over passive reading.
- AKT Navigator: questions, explanations and mock exams are free.
  Everything is free until 8 July 2026; after that, full audio access
  starts at £59 (Early Access, bought before 8 July) or £79 (standard).
- The audio library covers all 32 RCGP AKT curriculum topics in 90+ hours.

## Free demo (no signup)

- [Demo overview + worked example question](https://medexia-akt.com/demo):
  what the demo contains, plus a full sample AKT question with its
  structured explanation (understanding the question, key points, why
  the other options are wrong) rendered in plain text
- [Audio demo](https://app.medexia-akt.com/demo/audiobook): a real
  narrated sample from the 90+ hour library
- [Question demo](https://app.medexia-akt.com/demo/questions): five real
  AKT-style single-best-answer questions with structured explanations
  and results

Question standards: single-best-answer format matching the real exam,
aligned to NICE, CKS and the BNF, regularly reviewed and updated.

## Pages

- [Home](https://medexia-akt.com/): product overview, live demo, reviews, pricing
- [Demo](https://medexia-akt.com/demo): try the app free — audio sample + 5 real questions
- [How to pass the AKT](https://medexia-akt.com/how-to-pass-the-akt): study guide — format, pass rate, strategy, topic breakdown
- [AKT revision plan](https://medexia-akt.com/akt-revision-plan): 12-week, 8-week and 4-week AKT timetable for GP trainees
- [AKT exam dates](https://medexia-akt.com/akt-exam-dates): July and October 2026 sittings, booking windows, results dates, eligibility
- [AKT exam format](https://medexia-akt.com/akt-exam-format): 160-question format, section weighting, eligibility, marking and pass-mark rules
- [AKT exam day](https://medexia-akt.com/akt-exam-day): Pearson VUE test centre booking, required ID, arrival timing, personal belongings and reasonable adjustments
- [AKT exam fee](https://medexia-akt.com/akt-exam-fee): current RCGP AKT fee, booking, eligibility and attempt limits
- [AKT pass rate](https://medexia-akt.com/akt-pass-rate): official recent pass rates, pass marks and candidate numbers from RCGP feedback reports
- [AKT results and retakes](https://medexia-akt.com/akt-results-and-retakes): result reports, unsuccessful attempts, retake planning, appeals and attempt limits
- [AKT feedback reports](https://medexia-akt.com/akt-feedback-reports): latest RCGP feedback report weak areas and recurring revision themes
- [AKT statistics](https://medexia-akt.com/akt-statistics): evidence-based practice, data interpretation and key statistics formulas
- [AKT statistics formulas](https://medexia-akt.com/akt-statistics-formulas): NNT, NNH, sensitivity, specificity, PPV, NPV and risk formulas
- [AKT prescribing and medication safety](https://medexia-akt.com/akt-prescribing-and-medication-safety): medication monitoring, prescribing errors, side effects and BNF/NICE CKS checking
- [AKT confidentiality, safeguarding and data protection](https://medexia-akt.com/akt-confidentiality-safeguarding-data-protection): confidentiality, information sharing, recorded consultations, safeguarding and GMC guidance
- [AKT neurology](https://medexia-akt.com/akt-neurology): gait disturbance, headache red flags, seizures, stroke, Parkinson's, MS and neurology feedback themes
- [AKT children and young people](https://medexia-akt.com/akt-children-young-people): acute child illness, paediatric cancer red flags, safeguarding, immunisation and adolescent health
- [AKT dermatology](https://medexia-akt.com/akt-dermatology): rashes, eczema, psoriasis, acne, fungal infection, skin lesions and skin cancer recognition
- [AKT minor illness and urgent care](https://medexia-akt.com/akt-minor-illness-urgent-care): sepsis recognition, NEWS2, delayed antibiotics, common infections and safety-netting
- [AKT mental health](https://medexia-akt.com/akt-mental-health): depression, anxiety, suicide risk, self-harm, SSRI prescribing, psychosis and legal frameworks
- [AKT syllabus](https://medexia-akt.com/akt-syllabus): section weighting and all 32 RCGP curriculum topic guides
- [AKT mock exams](https://medexia-akt.com/akt-mock-exam): free timed mocks with AI debriefs
- [AKT audio revision](https://medexia-akt.com/akt-audio-revision): the 90+ hour audiobook library
- [AKT question banks compared](https://medexia-akt.com/best-akt-question-bank): how AKT Navigator compares
- [All 32 AKT topics](https://medexia-akt.com/topics): per-topic revision guides
- [FAQ](https://medexia-akt.com/faq): what the AKT is, pass rate, revision timeline, free access

## App

The product itself runs at https://app.medexia-akt.com (free account
required). Support: support@medexia-akt.com
